subject: Databases
title: Savepoints
description: A micro-lesson that makes Savepoints usable.

hook.text: A transaction has five steps. Step four fails. ROLLBACK undoes all five. But you only wanted to undo step four.

buildup.text: SAVEPOINT mid_point creates a checkpoint inside a transaction. You can roll back to it, not the beginning.

discovery.text: ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT mid_point undoes only the work after that savepoint. Earlier steps survive.

twist.text: Nested try/catch in app code maps naturally to savepoints. Catch an error, roll back the sub-operation, continue.

climax.text: Some ORMs use savepoints automatically for nested transaction blocks. Check your framework's behavior.

punchline.text: Savepoints: partial rollbacks inside a transaction. Granular control.

quiz.question: What does ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT do?
options: Undoes all work after the savepoint while keeping earlier work in the transaction intact
Rolls back the entire transaction to the very beginning
Deletes the savepoint and commits all changes
